---
title: "Final Bird Project"
author: "PMM"
format: pdf
editor: visual
---

### Trying to do gull things

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(lubridate)
library(adehabitatLT)
library(sf)           
library(data.table)   
library(tictoc)
library(ggplot2)
# ==============================================================================
# 1. DATA EXTRACTION & FILTERING
# ==============================================================================
raw_gull <- read_csv("FTZ_ Foraging site fidelity and spatial segregation in great black-backed gulls (data from Borrmann et al. 2019).csv") %>%
  mutate(timestamp = ymd_hms(timestamp), date = as_date(timestamp)) %>%
  group_by(`individual-local-identifier`) %>%
  filter(timestamp >= min(timestamp, na.rm = TRUE) + days(2)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(breeding_stage = case_when(
    format(timestamp, "%m-%d") <= "05-31" ~ "Incubation",
    format(timestamp, "%m-%d") <= "07-15" ~ "Chick-Rearing",
    TRUE ~ "Post-Breeding"
  )) %>%
  filter(breeding_stage != "Post-Breeding") %>%
  group_by(`individual-local-identifier`) %>%
  filter("Chick-Rearing" %in% breeding_stage) %>%
  ungroup()

# ==============================================================================
# 2. SPATIAL PROJECTION (Degrees -> Meters)
# ==============================================================================
# Convert raw lat/long into a spatial object, then project to UTM Zone 32N (Meters)
# This is mandatory for recurse to accurately calculate a 500m radius
gull_sf <- st_as_sf(raw_gull, coords = c("location-long", "location-lat"), crs = 4326) %>%
  st_transform(crs = 32632) # EPSG:32632 is UTM 32N

# Extract the new metric coordinates back into the dataframe
coords_utm <- st_coordinates(gull_sf)
raw_gull$x_utm <- coords_utm[,1]
raw_gull$y_utm <- coords_utm[,2]

# ==============================================================================
# 3. TRAJECTORY INTERPOLATION
# ==============================================================================
# Use the new metric coordinates for the trajectory
xy_data <- raw_gull[, c("x_utm", "y_utm")]
date_vec <- raw_gull$timestamp
id_vec   <- raw_gull$`individual-local-identifier`

# Store original metadata 
metadata_to_keep <- raw_gull %>%
  select(`individual-local-identifier`, timestamp, `ground-speed`, breeding_stage)

# Create and interpolate trajectory (1 point every 300 seconds / 5 mins)
gull_traj <- as.ltraj(xy = xy_data, date = date_vec, id = id_vec)
gull_interp <- redisltraj(gull_traj, u = 300, type = "time")

# Convert back to dataframe
raw_gull_interpolated <- ld(gull_interp) %>%
  rename(
    `individual-local-identifier` = id, 
    timestamp = date, 
    x_utm = x, 
    y_utm = y
  ) %>%
  mutate(date = as_date(timestamp))

# ==============================================================================
# 4. METADATA REJOIN (Rolling Join)
# ==============================================================================
# Convert to data.table for the nearest-neighbor join
setDT(raw_gull_interpolated)
setDT(metadata_to_keep)

# Set the exact columns to join on
setkey(raw_gull_interpolated, `individual-local-identifier`, timestamp)
setkey(metadata_to_keep, `individual-local-identifier`, timestamp)

# Perform the rolling join: grabs the metadata from the closest actual GPS ping
raw_gull_final <- metadata_to_keep[raw_gull_interpolated, roll = "nearest"]

# Convert back to a standard tibble/dataframe for the rest of your project
raw_gull_final <- as_tibble(raw_gull_final)

```

###Intertidal mudflats detection

```{r}

library(osmdata)
library(sf)
library(dplyr)
tic("tidal analysis")
# ==============================================================================
# 2A. MUDFLAT POLYGON EXTRACTION (VIA OSM)
# ==============================================================================
# 1. Define bounding box around the Föhr/Amrum Wadden Sea region
# (xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax)
study_area <- c(7.8, 54.2, 9.0, 54.9) 
#study_area <- c(8.2, 54.4, 8.8, 55.0) 

message("Downloading Wadden Sea tidal flats from OSM (Please wait 1-2 minutes)...")

# We only use the official 'tidalflat' tag to avoid the osmdata parsing bug
osm_tidal <- opq(bbox = study_area, timeout = 300) %>%
  add_osm_feature(key = 'wetland', value = 'tidalflat') %>%
  osmdata_sf()

# Extract and combine Polygons and Multipolygons
mudflat_poly <- bind_rows(
  osm_tidal$osm_polygons,
  osm_tidal$osm_multipolygons
) %>%
  # Filter out empty geometries
  filter(!st_is_empty(geometry)) %>%
  # Project to UTM Zone 32N (Meters) to match your GPS data
  st_transform(crs = 32632)

# ==============================================================================
# 2B. POINT-IN-POLYGON CALCULATION
# ==============================================================================
# Convert your interpolated GPS tracks into spatial points
gull_sf <- st_as_sf(raw_gull_final, coords = c("x_utm", "y_utm"), crs = 32632)

# Check which points fall inside the mudflat boundaries!
# This calculates the intersection and assigns TRUE/FALSE
message("Running spatial point-in-polygon math...")
raw_gull_final$in_mudflat <- lengths(st_intersects(gull_sf, mudflat_poly)) > 0

#Check how many points landed in the mud!
table(raw_gull_final$in_mudflat)

# Plot the first 1000 GPS fixes on top of the mudflats
# Use ggplot to verify your spatial data
ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = mudflat_poly, fill = "saddlebrown", color = NA) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Verified OSM Tidal Flats: Wadden Sea")
```

###recurse analysis for foraging ID

```{r}
library(recurse)
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(tictoc)

# ==============================================================================
# 2C. RECURSE ANALYSIS (Patch Identification)
# ==============================================================================

# 1. Run recursion analysis per bird to save memory
bird_ids <- unique(raw_gull_final$id)
gull_revisits_list <- list()

tic("revision recur")
for(bird in bird_ids) {
  message("Processing bird: ", bird)
  df_bird <- raw_gull_final %>%
    filter(id == bird) %>%
    # Thinning to 15 mins (every 3rd point) to save memory
    filter(row_number() %% 3 == 1) %>%
    select(x = x_utm, y = y_utm, t = timestamp, id = id) %>%
    as.data.frame()
  
  # Run recursion
  res <- getRecursions(df_bird, radius = 500)
  
  # SAFETY CHECK: Only store if revisits were found
  if(nrow(res$revisitStats) > 0) {
    gull_revisits_list[[bird]] <- res$revisitStats
  } else {
    message("No revisits found for: ", bird)
  }
}
toc()

gull_revisits_list <- gull_revisits_list[sapply(gull_revisits_list, nrow) > 0]

# 2. Use bind_rows correctly
# The .id argument will use the names of the list (your bird IDs) 
# to create the ID column automatically
all_revisits <- bind_rows(gull_revisits_list, `individual-local-identifier` = "individual-local-identifier") %>%
  select(`individual-local-identifier`, timestamp = entranceTime, residence_time_hrs = timeInside) %>%
  setDT()

# 3. Join back to your GPS data
# Since you have now standardized the ID column to 'id' in both tables:
setDT(raw_gull_final)
setnames(raw_gull_final, "individual-local-identifier", "id", skip_absent = TRUE)

setkey(raw_gull_final, id, timestamp)
setkey(all_revisits, id, timestamp)

# Redefine 'all_revisits'
all_revisits <- bind_rows(gull_revisits_list, .id = "id") %>%
  rename(start = entranceTime, 
         end = exitTime, 
         residence_time_hrs = timeInside) %>%
  setDT()


# non-equi join
raw_gull_final <- all_revisits[raw_gull_final, 
                               on = .(id, start <= timestamp, end >= timestamp), 
                               .(id, timestamp, x_utm, y_utm, 
                                 residence_time_hrs = residence_time_hrs)]

# Cleanup
raw_gull_final[is.na(residence_time_hrs), residence_time_hrs := 0]




# ==============================================================================
# 3A. DYNAMIC NEST LOCATION CALCULATION (The Geography Fix)
# ==============================================================================
# Find each bird's specific nest by calculating the median location 
# where they spend their nights during the Incubation phase.
nest_coords <- raw_gull_final %>%
  filter(breeding_stage == "Incubation", hour(timestamp) >= 22 | hour(timestamp) <= 3) %>%
  group_by(`individual-local-identifier`) %>%
  summarise(
    Nest_utm_x = median(x_utm, na.rm = TRUE),
    Nest_utm_y = median(y_utm, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
# Remove all the duplicate nest columns
raw_gull_final <- raw_gull_final %>%
  # Select only the columns you actually need
  select(
    `individual-local-identifier`, timestamp, `ground-speed`, breeding_stage, 
    x_utm, y_utm, residence_time_hrs, in_mudflat
  ) %>%
  # Re-join the clean nest coordinates just once
  left_join(nest_coords, by = "individual-local-identifier")


# ==============================================================================
# 3B. SEGMENTATION AND CLASSIFICATION
# ==============================================================================
raw_gull_final <- raw_gull_final %>%
  # 1. Distance Calculation (Now measuring from their UNIQUE nests!)
  mutate(
    dist_colony_m = sqrt((x_utm - Nest_utm_x)^2 + (y_utm - Nest_utm_y)^2),
    is_far = dist_colony_m > 500  
  ) %>%
  
  # 2. Sort strictly by time to ensure chronological order
  arrange(`individual-local-identifier`, timestamp) %>%
  
  # 3. Create Trip Breaks
  group_by(`individual-local-identifier`) %>%
  mutate(
    trip_break = (is_far != lag(is_far, default = FALSE)),
    segment_id = cumsum(trip_break)
  ) %>%
  
  # 4. Group by Segment to calculate Trip-Level Metrics
  group_by(`individual-local-identifier`, segment_id) %>%
  mutate(
    trip_dur_mins = as.numeric(difftime(max(timestamp), min(timestamp), units = "mins")),
    trip_n_fixes = n(),
    max_dist_km = max(dist_colony_m, na.rm = TRUE) / 1000,
    
    max_residence_hrs = max(residence_time_hrs, na.rm = TRUE),
    has_foraging_patch = max_residence_hrs >= 0.5, 
    
    used_mudflat = any(in_mudflat == TRUE & residence_time_hrs >= 0.5),
    
    valid_trip = (is_far == TRUE) & (trip_n_fixes >= 3) & (trip_dur_mins > 30) & (trip_dur_mins < 1440),
    
    # 5. Final Behavioral Classification
    trip_class = case_when(
      !valid_trip ~ "Colony/Rest/Transit",
      valid_trip & has_foraging_patch & used_mudflat ~ "Mudflat Foraging Trip",
      valid_trip & has_foraging_patch & !used_mudflat ~ "Other Foraging Trip (Pelagic/Inland)",
      valid_trip & !has_foraging_patch ~ "Search/Commute (No Patch Found)"
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# ==============================================================================
# 3C. EXTRACT ANALYTICAL DATASET
# ==============================================================================
foraging_data <- raw_gull_final %>%
  filter(trip_class %in% c("Mudflat Foraging Trip", "Other Foraging Trip (Pelagic/Inland)"))

# Check your recovered birds!
print(unique(foraging_data$`individual-local-identifier`))
```

###Tide analysis

```{r}
library(jsonlite)
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)

# ==============================================================================
# 4A. EXTRACT OFFICIAL DATUMS
# ==============================================================================
# Read the official Hörnum tidal data
hoernum_data <- fromJSON("hrnum-9570050-deu-wsv.json")

# Extract the limits
MHW <- hoernum_data$datums$MHW  
MLW <- hoernum_data$datums$MLW  

# ==============================================================================
# 4B. CALCULATE WATER LEVEL & CLASSIFY
# ==============================================================================
foraging_data <- foraging_data %>%
  mutate(
    # 1. Calculate the phase of the M2 lunar cycle (Period = 44712 seconds)
    tide_phase = (as.numeric(timestamp) %% 44712) / 44712,
    
    # 2. Simulate the water height (m) oscillating between official datums
    water_level_m = MLW + (MHW - MLW) * (0.5 * (1 + sin(2 * pi * tide_phase))),
    
    # 3. Classify into biological states (20cm buffer around extreme highs/lows)
    tide_state = case_when(
      water_level_m >= (MHW - 0.20) ~ "High Tide",
      water_level_m <= (MLW + 0.20) ~ "Low Tide",
      TRUE ~ "Transition"
    ),
    
    # Factorize for orderly plotting and modeling
    tide_state = factor(tide_state, levels = c("Low Tide", "Transition", "High Tide"))
  )

# ==============================================================================
# 4C. SUMMARIZE TRIP METRICS FOR GLMM
# ==============================================================================
# Distill the hundreds of pings into a single binary metric per trip
trip_tide_summary <- foraging_data %>%
  group_by(`individual-local-identifier`, breeding_stage, segment_id, trip_class) %>%
  summarise(
    total_fixes = n(),
    prop_high_tide = sum(tide_state == "High Tide") / total_fixes,
    
    # GLMM Response Variable: Did this trip occur primarily during High Tide?
    is_high_tide_trip = ifelse(prop_high_tide > 0.5, 1, 0),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Preview the final, model-ready dataset
head(trip_tide_summary)
```

###Calculating metrics

```{r}
library(dplyr)

# ==============================================================================
# 1. CALCULATE METRICS PER TRIP
# ==============================================================================
# We calculate max range, mudflat foraging time, and determine the dominant 
# tide state for every single valid trip.

trip_metrics <- foraging_data %>%
  group_by(`individual-local-identifier`, breeding_stage, segment_id, trip_class) %>%
  summarise(
    # 1. Maximal Distance (converted from meters to km)
    max_range_km = max(dist_colony_m, na.rm = TRUE) / 1000,
    
    # 2. Foraging Time in Mudflats (Mins)
    # Count fixes where the bird is in the mudflat polygon AND resident > 30 mins.
    # Multiply by 5 because our interpolated data is strictly 1 fix = 5 mins.
    mudflat_forage_mins = sum(in_mudflat == TRUE & residence_time_hrs >= 0.5, na.rm = TRUE) * 5,
    
    # 3. Dominant Tide State
    # Finds which tide state had the highest number of GPS fixes during this specific trip
    primary_tide_state = names(which.max(table(tide_state))),
    
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  # Ensure the tide states stay in logical order for plotting/modeling
  mutate(primary_tide_state = factor(primary_tide_state, levels = c("Low Tide", "Transition", "High Tide")))

# ==============================================================================
# 2. AGGREGATE TO INDIVIDUAL AVERAGES BY TIDE & BREEDING STAGE
# ==============================================================================
# Now we summarize the trip data to see how the "Parental Tax" and the "Tidal Cycle" 
# interact to shape the birds' overall effort.

indiv_tide_summary <- trip_metrics %>%
  group_by(`individual-local-identifier`, breeding_stage, primary_tide_state) %>%
  summarise(
    # A. Return Trips: How many trips were initiated under this tide state?
    total_trips = n(),
    
    # B. Mean Distance: How far do they fly under this tide state?
    mean_max_range_km = mean(max_range_km, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # C. Foraging Time: How much time is actually spent eating in the mud?
    mean_mudflat_time_mins = mean(mudflat_forage_mins, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# View the final analytical dataset
head(indiv_tide_summary)

```


###Statistical Analysis

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)

# Create a Pair ID column
# Assign 03 and 04 to the same group, others to their own unique ID
indiv_tide_summary <- indiv_tide_summary %>%
  mutate(
    pair_id = case_when(
      `individual-local-identifier` %in% c("GBBG-Foehr-2016-03", "GBBG-Foehr-2016-04") ~ "Pair_1",
      TRUE ~ paste0("Single_", `individual-local-identifier`)
    )
  )

# ==============================================================================
# 1. DATA PREPARATION & EXPOSURE TIME
# ==============================================================================
# Calculate exactly how many days each bird was tracked per breeding stage
tracking_effort <- raw_gull_final %>%
  group_by(`individual-local-identifier`, breeding_stage) %>%
  summarise(
    tracking_days = as.numeric(difftime(max(timestamp), min(timestamp), units = "days")),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Join tracking days to the summary dataset and set factor baselines
# Skip the join since it's already done! Just run the math and factor leveling:
# This keeps your original data and adds the tracking_days column to it
indiv_tide_summary <- indiv_tide_summary %>%
  left_join(tracking_effort, by = c("individual-local-identifier", "breeding_stage"))

indiv_tide_summary <- indiv_tide_summary %>%
  mutate(
    # Calculate raw rate for descriptive plotting later
    trips_per_day = total_trips / tracking_days,
    
    # Set statistical baselines to compare against Incubation / Low Tide
    breeding_stage = relevel(factor(breeding_stage), ref = "Incubation"),
    primary_tide_state = relevel(factor(primary_tide_state), ref = "Low Tide")
  )

# Verify it worked!
head(indiv_tide_summary$trips_per_day)

# ==============================================================================
# 2. STATISTICAL MODELING (GLMMs)
# ==============================================================================

# A. RETURN TRIP RATE (Poisson GLMM with Offset)
# Tests if chick-rearing increases trip frequency when accounting for days tracked
# Update your GLMMs to include the Pair ID
# This nests the individual within the pair, correctly accounting for 
# the fact that Bird 03 and Bird 04 share a nest and coordinate their trips.

trips_rate_model <- glmer(total_trips ~ breeding_stage * primary_tide_state + 
                            offset(log(tracking_days)) + 
                            (1 | pair_id / `individual-local-identifier`), 
                          data = indiv_tide_summary, 
                          family = poisson)

range_model <- glmer(mean_max_range_km ~ breeding_stage * primary_tide_state + 
                        (1 | pair_id / `individual-local-identifier`), 
                      data = indiv_tide_summary, 
                      family = Gamma(link = "log"))

time_model <- glmer((mean_mudflat_time_mins + 0.1) ~ breeding_stage * primary_tide_state + 
                      (1 | pair_id / `individual-local-identifier`), 
                    data = indiv_tide_summary, 
                    family = Gamma(link = "log"))

# 3. OUTPUT SUMMARIES
# ==============================================================================
cat("\n--- MODEL 1: RETURN TRIP RATE ---\n")
summary(trips_rate_model)

cat("\n--- MODEL 2: MAXIMAL DISTANCE ---\n")
summary(range_model)

cat("\n--- MODEL 3: FORAGING TIME EFFICIENCY ---\n")
summary(time_model)
``` 


### Data Visualizations matching the Mixed Models

```{r}
library(emmeans)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# ==============================================================================
# 1. GENERATE ESTIMATED MARGINAL MEANS (Model Predictions)
# ==============================================================================

# A. Trip Rate Predictions (Poisson model requires offset)
em_trips_daily <- as.data.frame(emmeans(trips_rate_model, 
                                        ~ breeding_stage * primary_tide_state, 
                                        offset = 0, # Standardizes to 1 day
                                        type = "response"))

colnames(em_trips_daily)[colnames(em_trips_daily) %in% c("rate", "response")] <- "daily_rate"
colnames(em_trips_daily)[colnames(em_trips_daily) %in% c("asymp.LCL", "lower.CL")] <- "lower"
colnames(em_trips_daily)[colnames(em_trips_daily) %in% c("asymp.UCL", "upper.CL")] <- "upper"

# B. Foraging Time Predictions (Gamma model)
em_time <- as.data.frame(emmeans(time_model, 
                                 ~ breeding_stage * primary_tide_state, 
                                 type = "response"))

colnames(em_time)[colnames(em_time) %in% c("rate", "response")] <- "response"
colnames(em_time)[colnames(em_time) %in% c("asymp.LCL", "lower.CL")] <- "lower"
colnames(em_time)[colnames(em_time) %in% c("asymp.UCL", "upper.CL")] <- "upper"

# C. Maximum Distance Predictions (Gamma model) - THIS IS NEW!
em_range <- as.data.frame(emmeans(range_model, 
                                  ~ breeding_stage * primary_tide_state, 
                                  type = "response"))

colnames(em_range)[colnames(em_range) %in% c("rate", "response")] <- "response"
colnames(em_range)[colnames(em_range) %in% c("asymp.LCL", "lower.CL")] <- "lower"
colnames(em_range)[colnames(em_range) %in% c("asymp.UCL", "upper.CL")] <- "upper"

# ==============================================================================
# 3. BUILD THE PLOTS (Optimized)
# ==============================================================================
# Custom color palette
col_pal <- c("Incubation" = "#4A90E2", "Chick-Rearing" = "#E67E22")

# We define the legend title once as a variable for consistency
leg_title <- "Breeding Stage"

p1 <- ggplot(em_trips_daily, aes(x = primary_tide_state, y = daily_rate, 
                                 color = breeding_stage, group = breeding_stage)) +
  geom_jitter(data = plot_data, aes(y = daily_trip_rate), width = 0.15, alpha = 0.3, size = 2) +
  geom_point(size = 4, position = position_dodge(0.3)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), width = 0.2, position = position_dodge(0.3)) +
  geom_line(position = position_dodge(0.3), linewidth = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = col_pal) +
  labs(title = "A) Foraging Trip Rates", y = "Daily Trips", x = "Tidal State", color = leg_title) +
  my_theme

p2 <- ggplot(em_time, aes(x = primary_tide_state, y = response, 
                          color = breeding_stage, group = breeding_stage)) +
  geom_jitter(data = indiv_tide_summary, aes(y = mean_mudflat_time_mins), width = 0.15, alpha = 0.3, size = 2) +
  geom_point(size = 4, position = position_dodge(0.3)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), width = 0.2, position = position_dodge(0.3)) +
  geom_line(position = position_dodge(0.3), linewidth = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = col_pal) +
  labs(title = "B) Foraging Time", y = "Time (min)", x = "Tidal State", color = leg_title) +
  my_theme

p3 <- ggplot(em_range, aes(x = primary_tide_state, y = response, 
                           color = breeding_stage, group = breeding_stage)) +
  geom_jitter(data = indiv_tide_summary, aes(y = mean_max_range_km), width = 0.15, alpha = 0.3, size = 2) +
  geom_point(size = 4, position = position_dodge(0.3)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), width = 0.2, position = position_dodge(0.3)) +
  geom_line(position = position_dodge(0.3), linewidth = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = col_pal) +
  labs(title = "C) Maximal Foraging Distance", y = "Range (km)", x = "Tidal State", color = leg_title) +
  my_theme

# ==============================================================================
# 4. COMBINE
# ==============================================================================
final_plot <- (p1 / p2 / p3) + 
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") & 
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") # This moves the collected legend to the bottom!

print(final_plot)
# Save a file with a much taller aspect ratio (height=14 instead of 12)
my_path <- file.path("G:", "My Drive", "שנה ב", "birds", "Final Project")
ggsave(file.path(my_path, "Gull_GLMM_Results.png"), plot = final_plot, width = 8, height = 14, dpi = 300)
```
